pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 17: report file is created at this exact path via heredoc
setup_task.sh line 13, export_result.sh line 8, verifier.py line 97 all reference this same path

Task description and setup_task.sh: PAGE 1 contains Bow-Tie diagram (line 24), PAGE 2 contains Risk Matrix (line 66). Verifier checks for >= 2 pages (verifier.py line 113)

setup_task.sh specifies: 1 Top Event (line 25-28) + 5 Threats (T1-T5, lines 33-37) + 5 Prevention Barriers (PB1-PB5, lines 42-46) + 5 Consequences (C1-C5, lines 51-55) + 5 Mitigation Barriers (MB1-MB5, lines 60-64) = 21 total shapes

setup_task.sh color specifications: #FF8800 for Top Event (line 27), #FF4444 for Threats (line 32), #4488CC for Prevention Barriers (line 41), #CC2222 for Consequences (line 50), #44AA44 for Mitigation Barriers (line 59)

privileged_info_summary: This task involves creating a bow-tie risk analysis diagram using diagrams.net. The task requires a 2-page diagram with 21 named shapes representing an offshore hydrocarbon release scenario. All data is synthetic, generated by setup_task.sh: 1 central Top Event (orange, #FF8800), 5 Threats (red, #FF4444) with 5 corresponding Prevention Barriers (blue, #4488CC), 5 Consequences (dark red, #CC2222) with 5 corresponding Mitigation Barriers (green, #44AA44). Page 2 contains a risk matrix showing these risks plotted on a Likelihood vs Severity grid. Output file paths are /home/ga/Diagrams/bowtie_hydrocarbon_release.drawio and /home/ga/Diagrams/bowtie_hydrocarbon_release.pdf.
